instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How Viable Is It For A Diabetic Person To Have A Term Insurance Plan?
A term insurance policy can help secure a family's financial future if the breadwinner passes away. This may be one of the prime reasons why individuals invest in a suitable term plan. But what if the individual is a diabetic? Can he/she still invest in term insurance for diabetic person?
Term Insurance for Diabetics
India is home to millions of people with diabetes, and the numbers are rising steadily. As per reports1, there were 77 million diabetics in India in 2019, and the number is expected to reach 134 million by the year 2045. As such, diabetes is a common ailment among many. But, this does not mean that diabetics can’t buy a term insurance plan.
Many insurance companies offer term insurance plans for diabetics2 and cover them against the risk of a premature demise. Usually, both Type I and Type II diabetics can invest in term insurance for diabetic persons for their coverage needs.
Why Should Diabetics Invest In A Term Insurance Plan?
Though diabetes might not be a terminal illness, it is an illness nevertheless. It can result in various health complications3. Moreover, in the case of severe diabetes, an individual might also die prematurely4.
Given these reasons, you may want to consider investing in a term insurance plan if you are a diabetic. The plan will cover the risk of premature death and secure the family’s financial future in your absence.
Important Aspects of Term Insurance For Diabetics
Though diabetics can enjoy a term life insurance plan, here are some aspects that should be kept in mind:
Impact on Premiums
Since the insured individual suffers from a known condition, the risk is high. As such, the insurance company may require an additional premium to cover the increased risk under term insurance for diabetic patients5. Thus, diabetics may have to shell out higher premiums for the term life cover.
Coverage Restrictions
Depending on the severity of the disease, the insurance company might restrict coverage6 for death due to the complications of diabetes in the term plan for diabetic patients. In such cases, death due to other reasons or causes will be covered, and diabetes-related death might be excluded.
Moreover, depending on the severity, the insurance company might also limit the coverage level for diabetics.
Pre-Entrance Health Check-Ups
Some insurance companies will insist on a pre-entrance health check-up to assess the severity of the illness before granting term insurance for diabetic patients7. You will, thus, have to undergo a health check-up before the policy is accepted and issued.
Diabetes Declaration
It is important to declare your diabetes when buying term insurance. In the proposal form, the insurance company asks about the medical complications you might suffer. In the medical section, you should inform the company about your existing diabetes. This will help the insurer assess the risk and issue the term insurance policy.
The declaration of diabetes is also important because insurance plans are contracts of utmost good faith. The insurance company believes that you have provided all the right information when buying the policy. If the insurance company finds out that you have distorted or not disclosed certain facts pertaining to your health, it may deny claim payment to your nominee. So, you should declare diabetes when buying a term plan so that the policy is not voided on the grounds of breaching good faith.
The Bottom Line
Diabetes is a common illness, and you need not fear the availability of insurance coverage. Life insurance companies grant coverage to diabetics, which is important given the medical impairment you have. So, know what term insurance is and invest in a suitable plan. With a comprehensive term plan for diabetic patients, you can secure your family’s future and get peace of mind too. So, invest in a suitable plan of term insurance for diabetics and stay financially protected.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. How Viable is it for a Person with Diabetes to Have a Term Insurance
Plan?
A term insurance policy can help secure a family's financial future if the breadwinner passes away. This may be one of the prime reasons why individuals invest in a suitable term plan. But what if the individual is having diabetes? Can he/she still invest in term insurance?
Term Insurance for Diabetics
In India, the number of diabetes patients are rising steadily. As per reports1, there were 77 million people with diabetes in India in 2019, and the number is expected to reach 134 million by the year 2045.
People who have been diagnosed with diabetes may find it difficult to get a life cover. They may feel vulnerable with no protection cover to fulfil their responsibility of financially safeguarding their families. Some insurance companies offer term insurance plans for people with diabetes2 and cover them against the risk of a premature demise subject to policy terms and conditions.
Why Should Person With Diabetes Consider Investing In A Term Insurance Plan?
Though diabetes might not be a terminal illness, it is an illness nevertheless. It can result in various health complications3. Moreover, in the case of severe diabetes, an individual might also die prematurely4.
Given these reasons, you may want to consider buying a term insurance plan if you have diabetes. The plan will cover the risk of premature death and secure the family’s financial future in your absence.
Important Aspects Of Term Insurance For Person With Diabetes
Though person with diabetes can buy a term life insurance plan, here are some aspects that should be kept in mind:
1. Impact on Premiums
Since the insured individual suffers from a known condition, the risk is high. As such, the insurance company may require higher premium to cover the increased risk under term insurance for person with diabetes5. 2. Pre-Entrance Health Check-Ups
Some insurance companies will insist on a pre-entrance health check-up to assess the severity of the illness before granting term insurance for a person with diabetes7. 3. Diabetes Declaration
It is important to declare your diabetes when buying term insurance. In the proposal form, the insurance company asks about your medical conditions. In the medical section, you should inform the insurance company about your existing conditions like diabetes. This will help the insurer to assess the risk and issue the term insurance policy. The declaration of diabetes is also important because insurance plans are contracts of utmost good faith. The insurance company believes that you have provided all the right information
while buying the policy. If the insurance company finds out that you have distorted or not disclosed certain facts pertaining to your health, it may deny claim payment to your nominee.
The Bottom Line
Know what term insurance is and invest in a suitable plan. With a comprehensive term plan for people with diabetes, you can secure your family’s financial future and get peace of mind too. So, consider investing in a suitable plan of term insurance and stay financially protected. The issuance of the policy considering the diabetes will depend on the underwriting guidelines of the insurance company and product terms and conditions.
